zh.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench是Henry del Grene of Isham和Mrs. Henry del Grene的兒子，於1310年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾易沙安。他於大約1340年與Amabel結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1361年在英國米德萊可倫敦被列為chief justice of the king's bench。他去世於1369年8月6日在英國諾薩普頓史爾布頓享年59歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾布頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench是Henry del Grene of Isham和Mrs. Henry del Grene的儿子，于1310年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾易沙安。他于大约1340年与Amabel结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1361年在英国米德萊可倫敦被列为chief justice of the king's bench。他去世于1369年8月6日在英国諾薩普頓史爾布頓享年59岁，埋葬在英国諾薩普頓史爾布頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Green
meanings: English: either a nickname for someone who was fond of dressing in this color (Old English grēne) or was young or immature, or who had played the part of the ‘Green Man’ in the May Day celebrations, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a village green (Middle English grene, a transferred use of the color term). This is one of the most common and widespread of English surnames. In North America it has assimilated cognates from other languages, notably German Grün (see Gruen ) and Dutch Groen ; compare 7 below. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: alternatively, from a Middle English personal name Grene.
Irish: adopted for Ó hUainín ‘descendant of Uainín’, a personal name from a pet form of uaine ‘green’, see Honan .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench was born in 1310, in Isham, Northamptonshire, England as the son of Henry del Grene of Isham and Mrs. Henry del Grene. He married Amabel about 1340. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. In 1361, his occupation is listed as chief justice of the king's bench in London, Middlesex, England. He died on 6 August 1369, in Boughton, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Boughton, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench est né en 1310, à Isham, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, fils de Henry del Grene of Isham et Mrs. Henry del Grene. Il a épousé Amabel environ 1340. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. En 1361, sa profession de son indiquée est chief justice of the king's bench à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 6 août 1369, à Boughton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Boughton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench wurde 1310 in Isham, Northamptonshire, England, als Sohn von Henry del Grene of Isham und Mrs. Henry del Grene geboren. Er hat um 1340 Amabel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1361 ist sein Beruf als chief justice of the king's bench in London, Middlesex, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 6. August 1369 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Boughton, Northamptonshire, England und wurde in Boughton, Northamptonshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench è nato il nel 1310 nel Isham, Northamptonshire, England come figlio di Henry del Grene of Isham e Mrs. Henry del Grene. Lui sposato Amabel circa 1340. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Nel 1361, la sua occupazione è indicata come chief justice of the king's bench nel London, Middlesex, England. Morì il 6 agosto 1369, nel Boughton, Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Boughton, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.Isham, Northamptonshire, England: イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアイシャム
ja.London, Middlesex, England: イギリスミッドレセックスロンドン

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Benchは1310年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアイシャムでHenry del Grene of IshamとMrs. Henry del Greneの息子として生まれた。約1340年にAmabelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1361年，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンでの職業はchief justice of the king's benchと記録されている。彼は1369年8月6日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアボウトンで59歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアボウトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench(은)는 1310년 영국 노르섬프토실르 이샤에서 Henry del Grene of Isham(와)과 Mrs. Henry del Grene의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1340년 Amabel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1361년, 그의 직업은 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 chief justice of the king's bench이었다. 그는 1369년 8월 6일 영국 노르섬프토실르 부그h턴에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 부그h턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench nasceu em 1310, em Isham, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra filho de Henry del Grene of Isham e Mrs. Henry del Grene. Ele casou-se com Amabel aproximadamente 1340. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Em 1361, sua profissão aparece como chief justice of the king's bench em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 6 de agosto de 1369, em Boughton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Boughton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench родился в 1310 г. в местности ишам, нортамптоншир, Англия. Его родителей звали Henry del Grene of Isham и Mrs. Henry del Grene. Он женился на женщине по имени Amabel около 1340 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. В 1361 году он работал по профессии chief justice of the king's bench в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. Он умер 6 августа 1369 г. в местности боугтон, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности боугтон, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Henry de Grene Chief Justice of the King's Bench nació en 1310, en Isham, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra como hijo de Henry del Grene of Isham y Mrs. Henry del Grene. Se casó con Amabel aproximadamente 1340. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. En 1361, su ocupación registrada era chief justice of the king's bench en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Murió el 6 de agosto de 1369, en Boughton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Boughton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
